entry.title: Wendy's Debuts New Thin Mints Frosty Fusion and Brings Back Thin Mints Frosty
entry.summary:
The Thin Mints Frosty is back on the menu at Wendy's and is now called the "Thin Mints Frosty Swirl." Joining it is the new Thin Mints Frosty Fusion.
The Thin Mints Frosty Swirl (you can find my review of it here) features a Vanilla Frosty swirled and topped with a minty cookie crumble sauce inspired by Girl Scouts Thin Mints cookies. The new Thin Mints Frosty Fusion (pictured) features a Vanilla Frosty mixed with a minty cookie crumble sauce and crushed Girl Scout Thin Mints cookies. Both desserts can also be made with a Chocolate Frosty instead of Vanilla and are available now for a limited time. Prices vary, but at a Wendy's here in Los Angeles, the Thin Mints Frosty Swirl ranges from $2.99 to $3.99, while the Thin Mints Frosty Fusion ranges from $4.19 to $5.49. Additionally, Girl Scouts will be selling cookies at participating Wendy's restaurants nationwide during National Girl Scout Cookie Weekend from February 20-22, and throughout the Girl Scout Cookie season. Photo via Wendy's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper Returns to Burger King
entry.summary:
The Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper is back for a limited time at Burger King.
The Whopper variant was introduced in 2024 and features a quarter-pound flame-grilled beef patty, a maple bourbon BBQ sauce, crispy-fried onions, crispy-fried jalapenos, maple candied bacon, and American cheese, served on a toasted sesame seed bun. A Whopper Jr. version is also available. Prices vary, but the Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper costs $7.99 near me in Los Angeles, while the Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper Jr. costs $4.79. There's also a Maple BBQ Solo Moment meal and Family-Style BBQ Bundle as digital-exclusive specials when ordering through the Burger King website or mobile app. The Maple BBQ Solo Moment includes a Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper, small French fries (or onion rings or Have-sies), a Cinnamon Apple Pie, and a small soda for $12 (may vary), while the Family-Style BBQ Bundles costs $27 and includes two Cheeseburgers, two Maple Bourbon BBQ Whoppers, four Cheesy Bacon Tots, two small orders of French fries, and two Cinnamon Apple Pie. Photo via Burger King. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jack in the Box Brings Back Hot Mess Cheeseburger
entry.summary:
Jack in the Box brings back the Hot Mess Cheeseburger for a limited time through April 5, 2026.
The Hot Mess Cheeseburger features a grilled beef patty, white cheese sauce, shredded pepper jack cheese, pickled jalapenos, and crispy-fried onion rings, served on toasted sourdough bread. A double version with two beef patties is also available. You can find my 2013 review of the Hot Mess Cheeseburger here. There's also a Hot Mess Munchie Meal (pictured) that includes the burger plus two tacos, seasoned curly fries, a drink, and a limited-edition Meat Riot antenna ball (the Hot Mess was introduced back in 2013 with advertising featuring an '80s rock band by the name of Meat Riot). The Hot Mess Cheeseburger starts at $5.99 (it's $6.99 here in Los Angeles), while the Hot Mess Munchie Meal starts at $13 ($14 near me). Photo via Jack in the Box. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Shamrock Shake and Oreo Shamrock McFlurry Return to McDonald's
entry.summary:
Ahead of St. Patrick's Day 2026, the Shamrock Shake and Oreo Shamrock McFlurry have returned to the McDonald's menu for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
The Shamrock Shake features vanilla soft serve blended with minty (and green) Shamrock syrup, topped with whipped cream. The Oreo Shamrock McFlurry, on the other hand, features vanilla soft serve mixed with Shamrock syrup and Oreo cookie pieces. Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles charges $4.39 for a medium Shamrock Shake and $4.59 for a regular-size Oreo Shamrock McFlurry. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Popeyes Adds New Flavored Shrimp and Brings Back Flounder Fish Sandwich
entry.summary:
Popeyes introduces new flavored Butterfly Shrimp and brings back its Flounder Fish Sandwich for the 2026 Lenten/seafood season.
While Popeyes' crispy-fried Butterfly Shrimp can be ordered in its original "Classic" flavor or in several flavors, including Garlic Parm, Lemon Pepper, Ghost Pepper, and Buffalo. They can be ordered as part of the Shrimp Tackle Box and Surf & Turf meal. The Shrimp Tackle Box includes eight Butterfly Shrimp in your choice of flavor served with a regular side, a biscuit, and your choice of dipping sauce. The Surf & Turf meal includes four Butterfly Shrimp and two Chicken Tenders served with a regular side, a biscuit, and your choice of dipping sauce. Prices vary, but both the Shrimp Tackle Box and Surft & Turf meal start at $6.99 at a local Popeyes here in Los Angeles. The Flounder Fish Sandwich features a crispy-fried Alaska flounder filet served with barrel-cured pickles and either Classic Tartar sauce or Spicy Spread on a toasted brioche bun. It costs $6.99 near me. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Banquet Mega Breakfast Bowls Arrive
entry.summary:
Conagra Brands, Inc. launches new Banquet Mega Breakfast Bowls, featuring 30g of protein per bowl.
The new frozen breakfast bowls are rolling out to stores this month and come in four varieties: - Meat Lovers (pictured) - Scrambled eggs, sausage, roasted potatoes, ham, bacon, cheddar cheese sauce, and cheddar cheese. - Bacon - Scrambled eggs, roasted potatoes, bacon, cheddar cheese sauce, and shredded cheese. - Sausage - Scrambled eggs, sausage, roasted potatoes, cheddar cheese sauce, and shredded cheese. - Sausage & Gravy - Scrambled eggs, sausage, roasted potatoes, country gravy, and shredded cheese. To preserve the various textures of the eggs, potatoes, and protein, the bowls feature a tray-in-tray steaming setup that keeps the sauce and ingredients separate during heating. Another Conagra brand, Healthy Choice, uses a similar setup for its Simply Steamers line. Banquet Mega Breakfast Bowls range from 9 to 10.5 ounces. Prices vary, but Walmart sells them for $4 each. Photo via Banquet. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Church's Offers $5 Big Deal Box
entry.summary:
The Big Deal Box arrives at Church's Texas Chicken as the fast-food fried chicken chain's latest meal deal.
Starting at $5, the Big Deal Box includes a choice of a leg and thigh or three tenders served with two regular classic sides, a Honey-Butter Biscuit, and a jalapeno pepper. A seafood version that swaps in eight Classic Shrimp and cocktail sauce in lieu of chicken is also available and starts at $6.49. If you can't decide between the two, there's also the Surf & Turf Big Deal Box, which includes a leg and thigh or two tenders, four Classic Shrimp, two regular classic sides, a Honey-Butter Biscuit, and a jalapeno pepper. Additionally, Church's continues to offer the Texas 2PC Feast and has brought back its Blueberry Cheesecake Fried Pie for a limited time. The Texas 2PC Feast starts at $3.49 and includes a leg and thigh or two tenders with dipping sauce, accompanied by a Honey-Butter Biscuit and a jalapeno pepper. Church's Blueberry Cheesecake Fried Pie is a turnover-style pie featuring a crispy, flaky crust and a combination of cheesecake filling and blueberry filling side by side. Prices may vary, but the pie costs $1.99 here in Los Angeles. Photo via Church's Texas Chicken. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Bakes New Biscoff Skillet Cookie
entry.summary:
Crumbl welcomes the new Biscoff Skillet Cookie to headline this week's limited-time selection of baked desserts. The full rotation available from February 16 through February 21, 2026, is as follows:
- Biscoff Skillet Cookie - A warm, lightly-spiced Biscoff cookie topped with a scoop of mousse, crumbled Biscoff pieces, and a drizzle of melty Biscoff cookie butter. - Raspberry Cheesecake - Vanilla cheesecake on a buttery graham cracker crust, topped with a tangy raspberry spread and a whipped cream rosette. - S'mores Cookie - A graham cracker cookie packed with milk chocolate chips, topped with a melty marshmallow, chocolate drizzle, and buttery graham cracker crumbs. - Buckeye Brownie Cookie - A warm chocolate brownie cookie topped with peanut butter and melted semi-sweet chocolate. Crumbl also offers a permanent Classic menu that includes: - Semi-sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie filled with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. - Pink Sugar Cookie - An almond sugar cookie topped with a pink almond frosting. - Chocolate Crumb Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm chocolate cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with vanilla cream cheese frosting and more Oreo cookie pieces. - Brownie Batter Cookie - A warm chocolate cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chips and topped with brownie batter. - Snickerdoodle Cookie - A warm vanilla sugar cookie rolled in a cinnamon-sugar coating. - Celebration Cake Cookie - A warm cake batter cookie topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting and rainbow sprinkles. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Ben & Jerry's Launches Three New Sundae Flavors
entry.summary:
Ben & Jerry's launches three new flavors of its grocery store line of Sundaes, which feature pints of ice cream topped with whipped topping and more.
The new Sundae flavors are rolling out to retailers now and include: - Straw-Bae Shortcake (pictured) - Sweet cream ice cream with shortcake cookies and strawberry swirls, topped with strawberry-swirled whipped topping and shortcake crumble. - PB Blondie Bestie - Peanut butter ice cream with blonde brownies and graham cracker swirls, topped with caramel-swirled whipped topping & mini peanut butter cups. - Mocha Mood Pie - Chocolate cold brew ice cream with chocolate cookies and chocolate cookie swirls, topped with fudge-swirled whipped topping and fudge chips. Ben & Jerry's Sundaes launched in 2025 with a lineup that includes Dulce De-lish!, Choco-lotta Cheesecake, Turtle, and the Cookie Vermont-ster flavors. Sundae pints can be found at grocery retailers for a suggested price of $4.99 to $6.49. Photo via Ben & Jerry's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jumbo Shrimp Returns to Del Taco
entry.summary:
Jumbo Shrimp has made its way back onto the Del Taco menu for the 2026 Lenten/seafood season.
Available now for a limited time through April 14, 2026, the fast-food chain is serving up Jumbo Shrimp Tacos and Jumbo Shrimp Burritos. Jumbo Shrimp Tacos are being offered at 2 for $6. Each one features crispy-fried Jumbo Shrimp, house-made pico de gallo, cabbage, and secret sauce, folded in a warm flour tortilla and served with a lime wedge. The Jumbo Shrimp Burrito features Jumbo Shrimp, cilantro-lime rice, pico de gallo, cabbage, and secret sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. Del Taco is also offering a limited-time deal on its Beer Battered Fish Tacos, where you can get 3 for $7. The fish taco features a beer-battered and fried wild-caught Alaska Pollock filet, shredded cabbage, secret sauce, and pico de gallo, folded in a warm corn tortilla, and served with a lime wedge. Photo via Del Taco. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Carl's Jr. Offers Buy-One-Get-One-for-$1 Deal on Famous Star Burger
entry.summary:
Carl's Jr. is offering a limited-time deal where you can get a Famous Star burger for $1 when you buy one at regular price at participating locations chain-wide.
The Famous Star is one of Carl's Jr.'s signature burgers (along with the Western Bacon Cheeseburger). It features a charbroiled beef patty, melted American cheese, lettuce, tomato, onions, dill pickles, Special Sauce, and mayo, on a toasted sesame seed bun. Prices vary, but a single-patty Famous Star (the promotion is only good on the single-patty version) costs $7.49 at my local Carl's Jr. in Los Angeles. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!
